claim: The title claims breakthrough without pore-network clogging.
boundary: Supported only for the current DEM parameter window, not a universal no-clogging law.

claim: Outlet breakthrough, local deposition and pore-network degradation are separated as non-equivalent observables.
boundary: Diagnostic separation is an interpretation of DEM/voxel evidence, not direct experimental observation.

claim: The study uses DEM debris tracking and DEM-derived pore reconstruction for a stiff 10,000-pebble Li4SiO4 bed with 1 mm pebbles.
boundary: DEM-derived pore reconstruction only; not experimental CT.

claim: Baseline porosity, largest connected void fraction and box-counting dimension are reported numerically.
boundary: Voxel-size-dependent topology metrics from the reconstructed DEM bed.

claim: Increasing Stokes drag-to-weight ratio shifts debris downstream and raises final BTC from zero to 0.0820.
boundary: Current drag cases only; no local pore-flow solution is claimed.

claim: Increasing debris count from 3000 to 10000 raises local blockage while the pressure-free clogging index stays very small.
boundary: Three single-seed loading states; no fitted loading law or critical transition is claimed.

claim: The abstract interprets the studied regime as pre-clogging migration/filtering, where weak breakthrough is a transport signal.
boundary: Interpretation is bounded by DEM conditions and does not establish a validated safety limit.

claim: The workflow combines local blockage, connected-pore loss and hydraulic consequence rather than relying on BTC alone.
boundary: Pressure response is future/calibration-facing; the current manuscript is not pressure-calibrated.

claim: The high-inventory localized-release case supports sparse-front saturation without outlet crossing while the bulk remains near the source.
boundary: Target-time high-inventory mechanism evidence; not an outlet-breakthrough or critical-clogging claim.
